title: Command Line Interface
text: analysiskmeans package can also accessed command line. example prompts shown ! get started, looking different commands can use package, use –help command. looking retrieve elbow plot various Kmeans clustering iterations, elbow command one use! following methods use elbow command: looking retrieve clustering kmeans plots various Kmeans clustering iterations, cluster command one use! following methods use cluster command: Let’s get started examples! Firstly, let’s install analysiskmeans. Also, read_data_file helper function load dataset csv/tsv format following example starts constructing input files! can continue writing inputs files tests/cli folder! Next, can make SingleCellExperiment object dataframes files! file fetched, object saved, can run functions start conducting analysis! can start using elbow plot function! also saving elbow plot image repository.
code: analysiskmeans --help Options:   -c, --counts <COUNTS>       Path to counts matrix (TSV/CSV, genes x samples)                               [default: ""] [type: string]   -gm, --genemeta <GENEMETA>  Path to sample gene metadata (TSV/CSV)                               [default: ""] [type: string]   -cm, --cellmeta <CELLMETA>  Path to sample cell metadata (TSV/CSV)                               [default: ""] [type: string]   -o, --output <OUTPUT>       Output directory [default: ""] [type: string]   -FALSE, --n-top <N-TOP>     Number of top variable genes                               [default: 50] [type: integer]   -mn, --min-k <MIN-K>        Minimum K Value [default: 5] [type: integer]   -mx, --max-k <MAX-K>        Number of top variable genes                               [default: 10] [type: integer] Options:   -c, --counts <COUNTS>           Path to counts matrix (TSV/CSV, genes x                                   samples)                                   [default: ""] [type: string]   -gm, --genemeta <GENEMETA>      Path to sample gene metadata (TSV/CSV)                                   [default: ""] [type: string]   -cm, --cellmeta <CELLMETA>      Path to sample cell metadata (TSV/CSV)                                   [default: ""] [type: string]   -o, --output <OUTPUT>           Output directory [default: ""] [type: string]   -FALSE, --n-top <N-TOP>         Number of top variable genes                                   [default: 50] [type: integer]   -mn, --min-k <MIN-K>            Minimum K Value [default: 5] [type: integer]   -mx, --max-k <MAX-K>            Number of top variable genes                                   [default: 10] [type: integer]   -mx, --selected-k <SELECTED-K>  A Selected K Value for Plotting                                   [default: 7] [type: integer] read_data_file <- function(path) {   ext <- tolower(tools::file_ext(path))   if (ext == "csv") {     utils::read.csv(path, row.names = 1, check.names = FALSE)   } else {     utils::read.table(path, sep = "\t", header = TRUE, row.names = 1,                       check.names = FALSE)   } } set.seed(42) num_genes <- 100 num_cells <- 20 #Used to be 8 raw_counts <- as.integer(rexp(num_genes*num_cells, rate = 0.1)) raw_counts <- matrix(raw_counts, nrow = num_genes, ncol = num_cells)  gene_metadata <- data.frame(   name = paste("Gene", 1:num_genes, sep = "_"),   length = as.integer(rnorm(num_genes, mean = 10000, sd = 500)) )  cell_metadata <- data.frame(   names = paste("Cell", 1:num_cells, sep = "_"),   batch = rep(1:2, each = num_cells/2),   label = rep(c("xylem", "phloem"), times = num_cells/2) ) #reading the files and beginning the CLI simulation   #Due to the vignette structure  counts_df <- read_data_file(paste(dirname(getwd()),"/tests/cli/counts_df.csv", sep = "")) genemeta_df <- read_data_file(paste(dirname(getwd()),"/tests/cli/genemeta_df.csv", sep = "")) cellmeta_df <- read_data_file(paste(dirname(getwd()),"/tests/cli/cellmeta_df.csv", sep = "")) sce1 <- SingleCellExperiment(   assays = list(counts = as.matrix(counts_df)),   rowData = genemeta_df,   colData = cellmeta_df ) results <- analysiskmeans::data_config(sce1) ## Warning in .library_size_factors(assay(x, assay.type), ...): 'librarySizeFactors' is deprecated. ## Use 'scrapper::centerSizeFactors' instead. ## See help("Deprecated") ## Warning in .local(x, ...): 'normalizeCounts' is deprecated. ## Use 'scrapper::normalizeCounts' instead. ## See help("Deprecated") sce1 <- results$sce mat_norm <- analysiskmeans::top_x_genes(sce1, n_top = 50) pca <- analysiskmeans::computepca(mat_norm) max_k <- 10 min_k = 5 outputs <- analysiskmeans::k_means(min_k = min_k, max_k=max_k, pca = pca) metrics<-outputs$metrics plot1 <- analysiskmeans::elbow_plot(metrics)

title: Package Functions
text: basic example use data_config function set SingleCellExperiment data usable functionality package!
code: ## Warning in .library_size_factors(assay(x, assay.type), ...): 'librarySizeFactors' is deprecated. ## Use 'scrapper::centerSizeFactors' instead. ## See help("Deprecated") ## Warning in .local(x, ...): 'normalizeCounts' is deprecated. ## Use 'scrapper::normalizeCounts' instead. ## See help("Deprecated")

title: Package Functions
text: Next, can take look creating subset data including particular number top selected genes! Keep mind fill parameter value n_top, 50 default value. small portion subset shown .
code: utils::data(example_sce, package="analysiskmeans")   sce <- example_sce   results <- data_config(sce) ## Warning in .library_size_factors(assay(x, assay.type), ...): 'librarySizeFactors' is deprecated. ## Use 'scrapper::centerSizeFactors' instead. ## See help("Deprecated") ## Warning in .local(x, ...): 'normalizeCounts' is deprecated. ## Use 'scrapper::normalizeCounts' instead. ## See help("Deprecated") sce <- results$sce   mat_norm <- top_x_genes(sce, n_top = 50) mat_norm[1:5,1:5] ##          [,1]     [,2]     [,3]     [,4]     [,5] ## [1,] 6.291017 0.000000 0.000000 3.364813 2.277725 ## [2,] 2.226863 5.094011 0.000000 0.000000 5.469322 ## [3,] 2.226863 2.166704 4.887945 3.951340 3.272103 ## [4,] 5.990491 4.978982 3.593868 5.331774 3.948061 ## [5,] 5.948110 4.062194 2.899177 1.023982 3.649314

title: Package Functions
text: can now compute PCA analysis part Kmeans. Within , need pass mat_norm object just created computepca() function. outputs pca function shown .
code: utils::data(example_sce, package="analysiskmeans")   sce1 <- example_sce   results1 <- data_config(sce1) ## Warning in .library_size_factors(assay(x, assay.type), ...): 'librarySizeFactors' is deprecated. ## Use 'scrapper::centerSizeFactors' instead. ## See help("Deprecated") ## Warning in .local(x, ...): 'normalizeCounts' is deprecated. ## Use 'scrapper::normalizeCounts' instead. ## See help("Deprecated") sce1 <- results1$sce   mat_norm1 <- top_x_genes(sce1, n_top = 50)   pca1 <- computepca(mat_norm1)   pca1$sdev[1:5] ## [1] 2.517584 2.402654 2.197769 2.060160 1.984220

title: Package Functions
text: kmeans function conducts clustering iterative fashion, going inputted parameters minimum k values maximum k values. km_list object contains large amount information clustering. metrics object contains wss average silhouette scores corresponding k value range. example metrics looks like shown .
code: utils::data(example_sce, package="analysiskmeans")   sce <- example_sce   results <- data_config(sce) ## Warning in .library_size_factors(assay(x, assay.type), ...): 'librarySizeFactors' is deprecated. ## Use 'scrapper::centerSizeFactors' instead. ## See help("Deprecated") ## Warning in .local(x, ...): 'normalizeCounts' is deprecated. ## Use 'scrapper::normalizeCounts' instead. ## See help("Deprecated") sce <- results$sce   mat_norm <- top_x_genes(sce, n_top = 50)   pca <- computepca(mat_norm)   max_k <- 10   min_k <-5   outputs <- k_means(min_k = min_k, max_k=max_k, pca = pca)   metrics<-outputs$metrics   km_list <- outputs$km_list   metrics ##    k      wss avg_silhouette ## 1  5 625.5026     0.06131297 ## 2  6 565.5828     0.06596026 ## 3  7 512.3719     0.06487671 ## 4  8 459.8210     0.06096352 ## 5  9 411.0190     0.05909937 ## 6 10 360.5383     0.06911235

title: Package Functions
text: cluster_plot() function takes outputs kmeans function plots data based principal components. example plot shown , cluster differentiated color. plot following based example selected k value. case chosen value 8 example.

title: This Package provides metrics and plots for a K Means Model
text: kmeans function conducts clustering iterative fashion, going inputted parameters minimum k values maximum k values. km_list object contains large amount information clustering. metrics object contains wss average silhouette scores corresponding k value range. example metrics looks like shown .
code: utils::data(example_sce, package="analysiskmeans")   sce <- example_sce   results <- data_config(sce)   sce <- results$sce   mat_norm <- top_x_genes(sce, n_top = 50)   pca <- computepca(mat_norm)   max_k <- 10   min_k <-5   outputs <- k_means(min_k = min_k, max_k=max_k, pca = pca)   metrics<-outputs$metrics   km_list <- outputs$km_list   metrics ##    k      wss avg_silhouette ## 1  5 625.5026     0.06131297 ## 2  6 565.5828     0.06596026 ## 3  7 512.3719     0.06487671 ## 4  8 459.8210     0.06096352 ## 5  9 408.9954     0.06596343 ## 6 10 360.5383     0.06911235

code: plot <- cluster_plot(selected_k=8, km_list, pca, results$cell_type) analysiskmeans --help Options:   -c, --counts <COUNTS>       Path to counts matrix (TSV/CSV, genes x samples)                               [default: ""] [type: string]   -gm, --genemeta <GENEMETA>  Path to sample gene metadata (TSV/CSV)                               [default: ""] [type: string]   -cm, --cellmeta <CELLMETA>  Path to sample cell metadata (TSV/CSV)                               [default: ""] [type: string]   -o, --output <OUTPUT>       Output directory [default: ""] [type: string]   -FALSE, --n-top <N-TOP>     Number of top variable genes                               [default: 50] [type: integer]   -mn, --min-k <MIN-K>        Minimum K Value [default: 5] [type: integer]   -mx, --max-k <MAX-K>        Number of top variable genes                               [default: 10] [type: integer] Options:   -c, --counts <COUNTS>           Path to counts matrix (TSV/CSV, genes x                                   samples)                                   [default: ""] [type: string]   -gm, --genemeta <GENEMETA>      Path to sample gene metadata (TSV/CSV)                                   [default: ""] [type: string]   -cm, --cellmeta <CELLMETA>      Path to sample cell metadata (TSV/CSV)                                   [default: ""] [type: string]   -o, --output <OUTPUT>           Output directory [default: ""] [type: string]   -FALSE, --n-top <N-TOP>         Number of top variable genes                                   [default: 50] [type: integer]   -mn, --min-k <MIN-K>            Minimum K Value [default: 5] [type: integer]   -mx, --max-k <MAX-K>            Number of top variable genes                                   [default: 10] [type: integer]   -mx, --selected-k <SELECTED-K>  A Selected K Value for Plotting                                   [default: 7] [type: integer] library(SingleCellExperiment) ##     get, grep, grepl, is.unsorted, lapply, Map, mapply, match, mget, ##     order, paste, pmax, pmax.int, pmin, pmin.int, Position, rank, ##     rbind, Reduce, rownames, sapply, saveRDS, table, tapply, unique, ##     unsplit, which.max, which.min  ## Loading required package: S4Vectors  ##  ## Attaching package: 'S4Vectors'  ## The following object is masked from 'package:utils': ##  ##     findMatches  ## The following objects are masked from 'package:base': ##  ##     expand.grid, I, unname  ## Loading required package: IRanges  ## Warning: package 'IRanges' was built under R version 4.5.2  ##  ## Attaching package: 'IRanges'  ## The following object is masked from 'package:grDevices': ## Welcome to Bioconductor ##  ##     Vignettes contain introductory material; view with ##     'browseVignettes()'. To cite Bioconductor, see ##     'citation("Biobase")', and for packages 'citation("pkgname")'.  ##  ## Attaching package: 'Biobase'  ## The following object is masked from 'package:MatrixGenerics': ##  ##     rowMedians  ## The following objects are masked from 'package:matrixStats': ##  ##     anyMissing, rowMedians read_data_file <- function(path) {   ext <- tolower(tools::file_ext(path))   if (ext == "csv") {     utils::read.csv(path, row.names = 1, check.names = FALSE)   } else {     utils::read.table(path, sep = "\t", header = TRUE, row.names = 1,                       check.names = FALSE)   } } set.seed(42) num_genes <- 100 num_cells <- 20 #Used to be 8 raw_counts <- as.integer(rexp(num_genes*num_cells, rate = 0.1)) raw_counts <- matrix(raw_counts, nrow = num_genes, ncol = num_cells)  gene_metadata <- data.frame(   name = paste("Gene", 1:num_genes, sep = "_"),   length = as.integer(rnorm(num_genes, mean = 10000, sd = 500)) )  cell_metadata <- data.frame(   names = paste("Cell", 1:num_cells, sep = "_"),   batch = rep(1:2, each = num_cells/2),   label = rep(c("xylem", "phloem"), times = num_cells/2) ) #reading the files and beginning the CLI simulation counts_df <- read_data_file(paste(getwd(),"/tests/cli/counts_df.csv", sep = "")) genemeta_df <- read_data_file(paste(getwd(),"/tests/cli/genemeta_df.csv", sep = "")) cellmeta_df <- read_data_file(paste(getwd(),"/tests/cli/cellmeta_df.csv", sep = "")) sce1 <- SingleCellExperiment(   assays = list(counts = as.matrix(counts_df)),   rowData = genemeta_df,   colData = cellmeta_df ) results <- data_config(sce1) sce1 <- results$sce mat_norm <- top_x_genes(sce1, n_top = 50) pca <- computepca(mat_norm) max_k <- 10 min_k = 5 outputs <- k_means(min_k = min_k, max_k=max_k, pca = pca) metrics<-outputs$metrics grDevices::png(paste(getwd(),"/tests/cli/plots/elbowplot.png", sep = ""), width = 8, height = 6, units = "in", res = 300) elbow_plot(metrics) message("Saved!") ## Saved!

title: Create final scatter plot of kmeans clustering — cluster_plot
code: library(analysiskmeans) data(example_sce) SingleCellExperiment::logcounts(example_sce) <- log1p(SingleCellExperiment::counts(example_sce))#Line taken from Claude AI - Debug sce <- example_sce results <- data_config(sce) #> Warning: 'librarySizeFactors' is deprecated. #> Use 'scrapper::centerSizeFactors' instead. #> See help("Deprecated") #> Warning: 'normalizeCounts' is deprecated. #> Use 'scrapper::normalizeCounts' instead. #> See help("Deprecated") mat_norm <- top_x_genes(results$sce, n_top = 50) pca <- computepca(mat_norm) outputs <- k_means(4, 9, n_starts = 25, seed = 42, pca) cluster_plot(7, outputs$km_list, pca, results$cell_type)

title: Compute Gap Statistic for each K — gap_statistic
code: library(analysiskmeans) data(example_sce) SingleCellExperiment::logcounts(example_sce) <- log1p(SingleCellExperiment::counts(example_sce))#Line taken from Claude AI - Debug sce <- example_sce results <- data_config(sce) #> Warning: 'librarySizeFactors' is deprecated. #> Use 'scrapper::centerSizeFactors' instead. #> See help("Deprecated") #> Warning: 'normalizeCounts' is deprecated. #> Use 'scrapper::normalizeCounts' instead. #> See help("Deprecated") mat_norm <- top_x_genes(results$sce, n_top = 50) pca <- computepca(mat_norm) outputs <- k_means(5, 9, n_starts = 25, seed = 42, pca) gap_statistic(pca, n_starts=25, 9, outputs$metrics) #>       logW   E.logW          gap     SE.sim #> 1 3.856946 3.936114  0.079167222 0.04078358 #> 2 3.772539 3.802685  0.030145701 0.04182158 #> 3 3.688131 3.697286  0.009155754 0.04347637 #> 4 3.611720 3.597200 -0.014519442 0.04656626 #> 5 3.533081 3.498033 -0.035047899 0.04918973 #> 6 3.446830 3.397636 -0.049194442 0.05228922 #> 7 3.360925 3.296461 -0.064463407 0.05450298 #> 8 3.267450 3.192973 -0.074477066 0.05692509 #> 9 3.164604 3.084985 -0.079619581 0.05893235
